data.narrative:
Newspace Research, a Series B defense and security frontier-tech company, has raised $73.2M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms operating in Defense & Security at similar or adjacent stages, including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy baseline, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $139M. Peer-median methodology anchors to six same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.2B and a range of $1.0B to $1.5B, directly implying $1.2B. The two methods diverge substantially, reflecting differences in methodology applicability: stage-progression is capital-efficiency focused, while peer-median reflects market pricing of comparable risk and growth profiles.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $118M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $670M. This wide range reflects material uncertainty around whether Newspace Research's capital efficiency and burn profile align more closely with typical venture-backed progression or with the premium valuations commanded by peer companies in this sector. Key uncertainties include revenue traction, customer concentration, technology differentiation relative to the peer set, and near-term funding needs.
